text: Continuous Integration, or CI, is a development practice where team members frequently merge their code changes into a shared repository. Each integration is verified by an automated build, which compiles the code and runs a suite of tests. This process is managed through CI pipelines, which execute these steps automatically every time new code is pushed. The main benefit is catching issues early, before they become more complex and costly to fix. By running tests immediately, developers can quickly detect and correct errors, ensuring that the main codebase remains stable and reliable for everyone.
